faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Farmingdale home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small holes in wool, silk, or cashmere garments, webbing in closet corners, silken tubes in fabric folds, and adult moths flying around bedrooms or closets. You may also notice larvae (small caterpillars) on clothing or shed larval skins. Our technicians provide thorough inspections to confirm the presence and extent of the infestation.

faqs.question: What factors affect the cost of clothes moth control in Farmingdale?
faqs.answer: Pricing between $550-$5,000 depends on the severity of the infestation, the size of your home, the number of affected rooms or closets, and the treatment method required. Minor infestations in a single closet cost less, while whole-home treatments or severe cases requiring heat treatment and multiple follow-up visits fall at the higher end. We provide free estimates after inspection.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment typically shows results within 24-48 hours for adult moths, but complete elimination requires addressing all life stages. The full treatment process usually takes 4-8 weeks with follow-up visits, as larvae and eggs require different approaches. We schedule strategic follow-ups to break the reproductive cycle and ensure your Farmingdale property remains moth-free.

faqs.question: Can I prevent clothes moths from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Yes! Prevention includes regularly cleaning closets, storing seasonal clothing in airtight containers, vacuuming frequently, using cedar blocks or lavender sachets as natural deterrents, and inspecting second-hand clothing before bringing it home. Our technicians provide customized prevention plans for your Farmingdale property and offer ongoing monitoring services to catch any new activity early.
